units.name: General Information and Concepts
units.description: Foundational computing concepts, hardware/software basics, data representation, operating systems, and development environments.

tasks.name: Computing Fundamentals
tasks.description: Identify roles of hardware, software, firmware, and the OS in running programs.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about hardware vs. software, operating system role, process vs. thread, and interpreters vs. compilers. Use entry-level NOCTI-style phrasing.

questions.prompt: Which component is considered hardware?
questions.correctAnswer: CPU

questions.prompt: Which best describes an operating system?
questions.correctAnswer: Software that manages hardware and runs programs
answers: Software that manages hardware and runs programs

questions.prompt: Which item is application software?
questions.correctAnswer: Spreadsheet program

questions.prompt: Firmware is typically stored in
questions.correctAnswer: Non-volatile memory

questions.prompt: A process is best defined as
questions.correctAnswer: An instance of a program in execution
answers: An instance of a program in execution

questions.prompt: A thread is best described as
questions.correctAnswer: A smaller unit of execution within a process
answers: A smaller unit of execution within a process

questions.prompt: Which translates high-level code to machine code all at once?
questions.correctAnswer: Compiler

questions.prompt: Which executes source code line by line at runtime?
questions.correctAnswer: Interpreter

questions.prompt: Which component temporarily stores data and instructions currently in use?
questions.correctAnswer: RAM

questions.prompt: Which statement about device drivers is correct?
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the OS to communicate with hardware
answers: They allow the OS to communicate with hardware

tasks.name: Data Types and Representation
tasks.description: Explain integers, floats, booleans, characters/strings, and how data is represented in memory (bits/bytes).
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) on primitive data types, literal vs. variable, type coercion, and memory units (bit/byte/KB/MB).

questions.prompt: Which data type represents whole numbers?
questions.correctAnswer: Integer

questions.prompt: Which value is a Boolean literal in many languages?
questions.correctAnswer: true

questions.prompt: A string literal is typically enclosed in
questions.correctAnswer: Quotes

questions.prompt: How many bits are in a byte?
Which best describes a variable?
questions.correctAnswer: A named storage location holding a value
answers: A named storage location holding a value

questions.prompt: Which is most likely a floating-point value?
Type coercion refers to
questions.correctAnswer: Automatically converting one data type to another
answers: Automatically converting one data type to another

questions.prompt: Which unit is largest?
questions.correctAnswer: MB

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a character?
Which best describes a literal?
questions.correctAnswer: A fixed value written directly in code
answers: A fixed value written directly in code

tasks.name: Programming Paradigms
tasks.description: Differentiate procedural, object-oriented, and event-driven programming at a high level.
tasks.genprompt: Create MCQs comparing procedural vs. OOP vs. event-driven styles, including real-world examples and when to use each.

questions.prompt: Which paradigm organizes code around functions and procedures?
questions.correctAnswer: Procedural

questions.prompt: Which paradigm models programs as interacting objects with state and behavior?
questions.correctAnswer: Object-oriented

questions.prompt: Button clicks and key presses primarily drive which paradigm?
questions.correctAnswer: Event-driven

questions.prompt: Encapsulation and inheritance are associated with
questions.correctAnswer: Object-oriented programming

questions.prompt: A game loop responding to user input and timers is an example of
questions.correctAnswer: Event-driven design

questions.prompt: Which is a typical benefit of OOP?
questions.correctAnswer: Code reuse through classes

questions.prompt: Procedural code is often structured as
questions.correctAnswer: A sequence of steps with function calls
answers: A sequence of steps with function calls

questions.prompt: GUI applications commonly use
questions.correctAnswer: Event-driven programming

questions.prompt: Which is most closely related to classes and objects?
questions.correctAnswer: OOP

questions.prompt: Which paradigm is best for simple scripts that run the same steps each time?
questions.correctAnswer: Procedural

tasks.name: Development Tools
tasks.description: Recognize IDEs, editors, version control systems, package managers, and CLIs.
tasks.genprompt: Write MCQs on the purpose of IDEs/editors, Git basics (commit, push, pull), and package managers (npm/pip) at a conceptual level.

questions.prompt: An IDE primarily provides
questions.correctAnswer: Integrated editing, building, and debugging tools
answers: Integrated editing, building, and debugging tools

questions.prompt: Which tool records changes to source code over time?
questions.correctAnswer: Version control system

questions.prompt: In Git, a commit is best described as
questions.correctAnswer: A snapshot of changes with a message
answers: A snapshot of changes with a message

questions.prompt: Which command uploads local commits to a remote repository?
questions.correctAnswer: Push

questions.prompt: Which pulls and integrates new commits from a remote?
questions.correctAnswer: Pull

questions.prompt: npm and pip are examples of
questions.correctAnswer: Package managers

questions.prompt: A CLI is best described as
questions.correctAnswer: A text-based interface to run commands
answers: A text-based interface to run commands

questions.prompt: Which file often lists project dependencies for a package manager?
questions.correctAnswer: A manifest like package.json

questions.prompt: Which best explains code linting?
questions.correctAnswer: Static checks for style and simple errors
answers: Static checks for style and simple errors

questions.prompt: Why write descriptive commit messages?
questions.correctAnswer: They help others understand what changed and why
answers: They help others understand what changed and why

tasks.name: Algorithms and Complexity (Intro)
tasks.description: Describe what an algorithm is and basics of efficiency (qualitative).
tasks.genprompt: Produce MCQs about what an algorithm is, input/output, and high-level time/space considerations (no math needed).

questions.prompt: An algorithm is best defined as
questions.correctAnswer: A step-by-step method to solve a problem
answers: A step-by-step method to solve a problem

questions.prompt: Inputs and outputs of an algorithm should be
questions.correctAnswer: Clearly defined

questions.prompt: Time complexity is a way to talk about
questions.correctAnswer: How running time grows as input size increases
answers: How running time grows as input size increases

questions.prompt: Space complexity refers to
questions.correctAnswer: How much memory an algorithm needs
answers: How much memory an algorithm needs

questions.prompt: Why do we compare algorithm efficiency?
questions.correctAnswer: To choose solutions that scale better
answers: To choose solutions that scale better

questions.prompt: A good algorithm should be
questions.correctAnswer: Correct and efficient

questions.prompt: Which is a qualitative sign of inefficiency?
questions.correctAnswer: The program becomes very slow as data grows a little
answers: The program becomes very slow as data grows a little

questions.prompt: A recipe in a cookbook is most similar to
questions.correctAnswer: An algorithm

questions.prompt: Which matters most when comparing two correct algorithms for large inputs?
questions.correctAnswer: How their performance grows with input size
answers: How their performance grows with input size
The font used to print code

questions.prompt: Which is a typical tradeoff when designing algorithms?
questions.correctAnswer: Time vs. space

units.name: Ethics, Legal Compliance, and Security
units.description: Professional conduct, intellectual property, privacy, and basic security practices for entry-level programmers.

tasks.name: Professional and Ethical Conduct
tasks.description: Understand ethics, professionalism, and responsible behavior in computing.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about professional ethics scenarios, respectful communication, conflicts of interest, plagiarism, and reporting concerns.

questions.prompt: Which action best demonstrates professional conduct on a software team?
questions.correctAnswer: Giving clear status updates and meeting deadlines
answers: Posting memes in project chats during meetings
Giving clear status updates and meeting deadlines

questions.prompt: A classmate asks for a copy of your project so they can submit it as their own. The ethical response is to
questions.correctAnswer: Refuse and suggest they do their own work
answers: Share the code and change filenames
Post the code publicly to help everyone
Refuse and suggest they do their own work

questions.prompt: Which is an example of plagiarism in programming?
questions.correctAnswer: Copying code without attribution and claiming authorship
answers: Copying code without attribution and claiming authorship
Citing a library’s license in your README
Using open-source code with proper attribution

questions.prompt: A conflict of interest occurs when a developer
questions.correctAnswer: Has personal interests that could improperly influence decisions
answers: Uses a linter on their code
Works from home one day a week
Has personal interests that could improperly influence decisions

questions.prompt: Which communication is most appropriate in a professional setting?
questions.correctAnswer: Respectful, concise, and relevant to the work
answers: Respectful, concise, and relevant to the work
Long messages with unrelated personal stories

questions.prompt: If you observe harassment or discrimination on your team, the best first step is to
questions.correctAnswer: Report it through the appropriate school/company channel
answers: Joke about it to defuse tension
Report it through the appropriate school/company channel
Ignore it if it doesn’t affect your work
Post about it on social media

questions.prompt: Which behavior supports an inclusive environment?
questions.correctAnswer: Listening actively and inviting all voices to contribute
answers: Listening actively and inviting all voices to contribute

questions.prompt: When discussing sensitive project details, you should
questions.correctAnswer: Share only with those who need to know
answers: Upload to public forums for feedback
Share only with those who need to know

questions.prompt: Ethical use of AI coding tools means
questions.correctAnswer: Following school/company rules and citing generated code if required
answers: Submitting AI output as your own without review
Following school/company rules and citing generated code if required

questions.prompt: Professional responsibility in a code review includes
questions.correctAnswer: Giving specific, respectful feedback focused on the code
answers: Giving specific, respectful feedback focused on the code
Rejecting all changes to keep control

tasks.name: Intellectual Property and Licensing
tasks.description: Basics of copyright, licensing, attribution, and using third-party assets.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) on copyright vs. license, attribution, open-source license characteristics (MIT/Apache/GPL at a high level), and proper reuse of code and media.

questions.prompt: Copyright primarily protects
questions.correctAnswer: Original works like code, text, images, and music
answers: Original works like code, text, images, and music

questions.prompt: A software license
questions.correctAnswer: Specifies how others may use, modify, and share the work
answers: Is the same as a trademark
Specifies how others may use, modify, and share the work
Prevents others from viewing the code

questions.prompt: Using code from the internet ethically means you should
questions.correctAnswer: Follow the license and provide attribution if required
answers: Follow the license and provide attribution if required
Copy it verbatim and remove license text
Assume all code online is public domain

questions.prompt: The MIT License is known for being
questions.correctAnswer: Permissive with minimal restrictions

questions.prompt: GPL-licensed code typically requires that
questions.correctAnswer: Derivative works are shared under the same license
answers: Derivative works are shared under the same license
You may not distribute source code
You must keep the code private

questions.prompt: Attribution means
questions.correctAnswer: Giving credit to the original creator as required by the license
answers: Paying a fee for every use
Giving credit to the original creator as required by the license

questions.prompt: If a project has no license, the safest assumption is
questions.correctAnswer: You do not have permission to reuse it
answers: You do not have permission to reuse it
It is covered by MIT License

questions.prompt: A proprietary EULA typically
questions.correctAnswer: Limits copying and redistribution

questions.prompt: Which action best avoids license conflicts?
questions.correctAnswer: Track third-party components and their licenses
answers: Track third-party components and their licenses

questions.prompt: When using open images or sounds in an app, you should first
questions.correctAnswer: Check the asset’s license and attribution requirements
answers: Assume school projects need no attribution
Only credit if the author emails you
Check the asset’s license and attribution requirements
Change the filename to be safe

tasks.name: Data Privacy and Compliance (Intro)
tasks.description: Identify personal data, minimize collection, and handle information responsibly.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about personally identifiable information (PII), data minimization, consent, retention, and safe sharing in school projects.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is PII (personally identifiable information)?
questions.correctAnswer: A student’s full name and school ID together
answers: A student’s full name and school ID together
An anonymized dataset with no keys

questions.prompt: Data minimization means
questions.correctAnswer: Collecting only the data needed for the stated purpose
answers: Collecting as much data as possible
Collecting only the data needed for the stated purpose
Sharing data with all team members

questions.prompt: Before sharing a class project dataset containing names and emails, you should
questions.correctAnswer: Remove or de-identify personal information where possible
answers: Upload it publicly to get feedback
Remove or de-identify personal information where possible
Share it in a group chat
Attach it to a public bug report

questions.prompt: Storing passwords in plain text is unsafe because
questions.correctAnswer: Anyone who accesses storage can read them directly
answers: Anyone who accesses storage can read them directly

questions.prompt: A good practice for data retention is to
questions.correctAnswer: Delete data when it is no longer needed
answers: Delete data when it is no longer needed
Keep data forever in case it’s useful
Back up PII on personal devices

questions.prompt: Sending PII over unsecured public Wi-Fi is risky because
questions.correctAnswer: It can be intercepted by others on the network
answers: It can be intercepted by others on the network

questions.prompt: When collecting survey responses from students, you should first
questions.correctAnswer: Explain the purpose and what data will be collected
answers: Explain the purpose and what data will be collected

questions.prompt: An anonymized dataset is one where
questions.correctAnswer: Personal identifiers have been removed so individuals aren’t identified
answers: Personal identifiers have been removed so individuals aren’t identified
Passwords are stored in plain text

questions.prompt: Sharing class data with another team should be done
questions.correctAnswer: Only if they need it and it’s approved
answers: Only if they need it and it’s approved

questions.prompt: Which is the safest place for sensitive project notes?
questions.correctAnswer: A private, access-controlled location

tasks.name: Security Fundamentals
tasks.description: Basic security principles for entry-level developers.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about authentication vs. authorization, least privilege, strong passwords, MFA, patching, and safe credential handling.

questions.prompt: Authentication primarily answers
questions.correctAnswer: Who are you?

questions.prompt: Authorization primarily answers
questions.correctAnswer: What are you allowed to do?
answers: What are you allowed to do?

questions.prompt: The principle of least privilege means
questions.correctAnswer: Give only the access required to perform a task
answers: Give only the access required to perform a task
Give admin rights to all students

questions.prompt: Which password is strongest?
questions.correctAnswer: A long passphrase with mixed characters
answers: A long passphrase with mixed characters

questions.prompt: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) increases security by
questions.correctAnswer: Requiring two or more independent proofs of identity
answers: Requiring two or more independent proofs of identity

questions.prompt: Storing API keys in source code is risky because
questions.correctAnswer: They can leak if the code is shared
answers: They can leak if the code is shared

questions.prompt: Keeping software up to date helps by
questions.correctAnswer: Applying security patches for known vulnerabilities
answers: Applying security patches for known vulnerabilities

questions.prompt: If you suspect an account is compromised, you should first
questions.correctAnswer: Change the password and notify the appropriate contact
answers: Change the password and notify the appropriate contact

questions.prompt: Sharing accounts among team members is discouraged because
questions.correctAnswer: It prevents traceability and increases risk
answers: It prevents traceability and increases risk

questions.prompt: A secure way to handle secrets in a project is to
questions.correctAnswer: Use environment variables or a secrets manager
answers: Use environment variables or a secrets manager

tasks.name: Safe Web Practices
tasks.description: Basic web safety concepts for entry-level projects.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about HTTPS, input validation concept, safe downloads, avoiding risky links, and safe behavior on shared devices.

questions.prompt: HTTPS helps protect users by
questions.correctAnswer: Encrypting data between browser and server
answers: Encrypting data between browser and server

questions.prompt: A safe practice when downloading software is to
questions.correctAnswer: Download from official or trusted sources
answers: Download from official or trusted sources

questions.prompt: Input validation helps prevent
questions.correctAnswer: Unintended or harmful input from being processed
answers: Unintended or harmful input from being processed

questions.prompt: On a shared computer, you should always
questions.correctAnswer: Log out of accounts and close the browser
answers: Log out of accounts and close the browser

questions.prompt: A suspicious link in an email should be
questions.correctAnswer: Not clicked; verify with the sender through another channel
answers: Not clicked; verify with the sender through another channel

questions.prompt: Public Wi-Fi is riskier for sign-ins because
questions.correctAnswer: Attackers may intercept traffic on the same network
answers: Attackers may intercept traffic on the same network

questions.prompt: Client-side code should not contain
questions.correctAnswer: Hard-coded passwords or API keys

questions.prompt: When a browser shows a certificate warning, you should
questions.correctAnswer: Avoid proceeding until the issue is resolved
answers: Avoid proceeding until the issue is resolved

questions.prompt: Using unique passwords for different sites is important because
questions.correctAnswer: A breach on one site won’t compromise others
answers: A breach on one site won’t compromise others

questions.prompt: Before entering credentials on a site, a good check is to
questions.correctAnswer: Verify the URL and the HTTPS lock icon
answers: Verify the URL and the HTTPS lock icon
Trust any link sent by text

units.name: Analyze Programming Problems, Manage Projects, and Create Flowchart Solutions
units.description: Requirements gathering, task breakdown, scheduling, and visual problem-solving with flowcharts and pseudocode.

tasks.name: Requirements and Use Cases
tasks.description: Identify stakeholders, separate functional vs. nonfunctional requirements, and draft simple user stories/use cases.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) on stakeholders, functional vs. nonfunctional requirements, acceptance criteria, and basic use-case thinking (entry-level NOCTI style).

questions.prompt: Which statement is a functional requirement for a login page?
questions.correctAnswer: The system shall allow users to sign in with a valid password
answers: The page should look modern on all screens
The page should load in under 1 second
The system shall allow users to sign in with a valid password
The color scheme should match school colors

questions.prompt: Which is a nonfunctional requirement?
questions.correctAnswer: Responses must be returned within 2 seconds
answers: Responses must be returned within 2 seconds

questions.prompt: A stakeholder is best defined as
questions.correctAnswer: Anyone affected by or invested in the system
answers: Anyone affected by or invested in the system
Only end users in the same class

questions.prompt: Which user story follows the standard format?
questions.correctAnswer: As a student, I want to view my grades so I can track progress
answers: I want grades to be fast
As a student, I want to view my grades so I can track progress

questions.prompt: Acceptance criteria are used to
questions.correctAnswer: Confirm that a feature works as intended
answers: Confirm that a feature works as intended

questions.prompt: Which requirement is most testable?
questions.correctAnswer: Export must generate a CSV file with headers name and score
answers: Export must generate a CSV file with headers name and score

questions.prompt: Gathering requirements early helps primarily to
questions.correctAnswer: Reduce changes and rework later

questions.prompt: Which is the best clarifying question for a teacher stakeholder?
questions.correctAnswer: Which grade fields do you need on the report?
answers: Which grade fields do you need on the report?

questions.prompt: A use case primarily describes
questions.correctAnswer: How a user interacts with the system to reach a goal
answers: How a user interacts with the system to reach a goal

questions.prompt: Which item belongs in a requirements list for a school portal?
questions.correctAnswer: Parents can view student attendance records
answers: Parents can view student attendance records
Write code in only one file

tasks.name: Project Planning Basics
tasks.description: Define scope, create milestones, estimate effort, and understand dependencies at a beginner level.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about scope vs. scope creep, milestones vs. tasks, estimates, priorities, and simple schedules (entry-level NOCTI style).

questions.prompt: Scope creep happens when
questions.correctAnswer: New features are added without adjusting time or resources
answers: New features are added without adjusting time or resources

questions.prompt: A milestone is best described as
questions.correctAnswer: A significant checkpoint or deliverable

questions.prompt: Which item is a task rather than a milestone?
questions.correctAnswer: Implement grade export button

questions.prompt: A dependency means
questions.correctAnswer: One task must finish before another can start
answers: Tasks happen at the same time
One task must finish before another can start

questions.prompt: Time estimation should be
questions.correctAnswer: Based on task size and team experience
answers: Based on task size and team experience

questions.prompt: The critical path in a schedule affects
questions.correctAnswer: The minimum time to finish the project
answers: The minimum time to finish the project

questions.prompt: Prioritizing tasks helps mainly to
questions.correctAnswer: Deliver the most important features first
answers: Deliver the most important features first

questions.prompt: A simple schedule should include
questions.correctAnswer: Tasks, owners, and target dates

questions.prompt: Free slack (float) for a task means
questions.correctAnswer: How much a task can slip without delaying the project
answers: How much a task can slip without delaying the project

questions.prompt: When a stakeholder asks for a big new feature mid-project, the best response is to
questions.correctAnswer: Discuss impact on schedule and scope before committing
answers: Discuss impact on schedule and scope before committing

tasks.name: Flowcharts and Pseudocode
tasks.description: Translate problems into simple flowcharts and pseudocode and interpret basic control flow.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about flowchart symbols (start/end, process, decision, input/output), sequence vs. selection vs. iteration, and interpreting short pseudocode in one line (no multi-line).

questions.prompt: In a flowchart, the oval shape typically represents
questions.correctAnswer: Start/End

questions.prompt: In a flowchart, a diamond indicates
questions.correctAnswer: A decision with branches

questions.prompt: Which symbol usually represents a process step?
questions.correctAnswer: Rectangle

questions.prompt: A parallelogram in a flowchart often means
questions.correctAnswer: Input or output

questions.prompt: Sequence, selection, and iteration are the three basic
questions.correctAnswer: Control structures

questions.prompt: Which line of pseudocode shows selection?
questions.correctAnswer: IF score >= 60 THEN pass ELSE fail
answers: IF score >= 60 THEN pass ELSE fail

questions.prompt: Which line of pseudocode shows iteration?
questions.correctAnswer: FOR i FROM 1 TO 3 DO total = total + i
answers: IF x < y THEN x = y
FOR i FROM 1 TO 3 DO total = total + i

questions.prompt: Which best describes pseudocode?
questions.correctAnswer: Plain-language steps resembling code logic

questions.prompt: A connector in a flowchart is used to
questions.correctAnswer: Link separate parts of the diagram
answers: Link separate parts of the diagram

questions.prompt: Which statement is most appropriate inside a process box?
questions.correctAnswer: total = total + amount

tasks.name: Risk and Constraints
tasks.description: Recognize schedule, technical, and resource risks and choose simple mitigations.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about identifying common project risks (schedule, staffing, dependency, technical uncertainty) and basic mitigation or contingency planning.

questions.prompt: A schedule risk is best described as
questions.correctAnswer: The project may take longer than planned
answers: The project may take longer than planned
The code compiles on first try

questions.prompt: A dependency risk occurs when
questions.correctAnswer: Your task is blocked until another task finishes
answers: Your task is blocked until another task finishes

questions.prompt: A technical risk example is
questions.correctAnswer: Using a new library with unknown issues
answers: The printer is out of paper
Using a new library with unknown issues

questions.prompt: Which is a basic mitigation strategy?
questions.correctAnswer: Create a backup plan for critical tasks
answers: Create a backup plan for critical tasks

questions.prompt: If one teammate with unique knowledge is absent, the risk is
questions.correctAnswer: Knowledge bottleneck

questions.prompt: A constraint in a school project is
questions.correctAnswer: Fixed due date for presentation

questions.prompt: Adding more features near the deadline mostly increases
questions.correctAnswer: Schedule risk

questions.prompt: A contingency plan is
questions.correctAnswer: Actions you will take if a risk happens
answers: Actions you will take if a risk happens

questions.prompt: Testing early reduces which risk most?
questions.correctAnswer: Finding defects too late to fix
answers: Finding defects too late to fix

questions.prompt: If a critical dependency is delayed, the best first step is to
questions.correctAnswer: Re-plan schedule and communicate impact

tasks.name: Team Roles and Communication
tasks.description: Understand basic roles (dev, tester, reviewer) and give clear status updates and handoffs.
tasks.genprompt: Generate multiple-choice questions (4 options) about roles and responsibilities, stand-up style updates (yesterday/today/blockers), handoff best practices, and constructive feedback.

questions.prompt: A typical developer role includes
questions.correctAnswer: Implementing features according to requirements

questions.prompt: A tester primarily focuses on
questions.correctAnswer: Verifying that the software works as expected
answers: Verifying that the software works as expected

questions.prompt: A code reviewer’s main goal is to
questions.correctAnswer: Improve quality by checking correctness and clarity
answers: Improve quality by checking correctness and clarity

questions.prompt: A clear stand-up update usually includes
questions.correctAnswer: What you did, what you will do, and blockers
answers: What you did, what you will do, and blockers

questions.prompt: Which is the best blocker description?
questions.correctAnswer: Waiting for API access to test login feature
answers: Waiting for API access to test login feature

questions.prompt: A good handoff note to a teammate should
questions.correctAnswer: Explain current status and next steps
answers: Explain current status and next steps

questions.prompt: Constructive code feedback should be
questions.correctAnswer: Specific, respectful, and actionable

questions.prompt: If two teammates disagree on an approach, the best next step is to
questions.correctAnswer: Compare options against requirements and constraints
answers: Compare options against requirements and constraints

questions.prompt: During a team demo, the presenter should
questions.correctAnswer: Show features that meet acceptance criteria
answers: Read source code line by line
Show features that meet acceptance criteria

questions.prompt: A clear task assignment includes
questions.correctAnswer: Owner, description, and due date

units.name: Design Program Solutions
units.description: Apply modular design, data structures, and simple interfaces to plan solutions before coding.

tasks.name: Modular Decomposition
tasks.description: Break problems into small, single-purpose functions and modules.
tasks.genprompt: Generate MCQs (4 options) on cohesion, coupling (qualitative), choosing function boundaries, and separating concerns at an entry level.

questions.prompt: Which is the best reason to split a long function into smaller ones?
questions.correctAnswer: Improves readability and reuse

questions.prompt: High cohesion in a module means
questions.correctAnswer: It does one focused job well
answers: It depends on many other files
It does one focused job well

questions.prompt: Low coupling between modules means
questions.correctAnswer: Modules have few dependencies on each other
answers: Modules have few dependencies on each other
Everything uses the same global state

questions.prompt: Which function boundary is most appropriate?
questions.correctAnswer: Separate input parsing from calculation
answers: Mix printing with math and file IO

questions.prompt: A benefit of modular design is
questions.correctAnswer: Easier testing of parts in isolation
answers: Easier testing of parts in isolation

questions.prompt: Which task should be its own function?
questions.correctAnswer: Format a score into a letter grade
answers: Format a score into a letter grade

questions.prompt: Separation of concerns means
questions.correctAnswer: Keep unrelated responsibilities in different parts
answers: Put all logic in one place
Keep unrelated responsibilities in different parts

questions.prompt: Which design is easier to change later?
questions.correctAnswer: Small functions with clear inputs and outputs
answers: Small functions with clear inputs and outputs

questions.prompt: When two functions share the same code block, a good step is to
questions.correctAnswer: Extract it into a helper function
answers: Extract it into a helper function

questions.prompt: A module that handles only file loading shows
questions.correctAnswer: Good cohesion

tasks.name: Data Modeling
tasks.description: Choose basic data structures that fit the problem.
tasks.genprompt: Generate MCQs (4 options) selecting between scalars, lists, and dictionaries for simple scenarios like inventory, leaderboard, and settings.

questions.prompt: Best structure to store ordered test scores for one student?
questions.correctAnswer: List

questions.prompt: Best structure for mapping student name to grade?
questions.correctAnswer: Dictionary

questions.prompt: Best way to store a single classroom temperature reading?
questions.correctAnswer: Number scalar

questions.prompt: To store multiple choice answers in order, use a
questions.correctAnswer: List

questions.prompt: App settings like {'volume':50,'theme':'dark'} should be a
questions.correctAnswer: Dictionary

questions.prompt: A leaderboard of top 5 scores in descending order is best as
questions.correctAnswer: List sorted by score

questions.prompt: Which is best for a schedule of class periods in sequence?
questions.correctAnswer: List of periods

questions.prompt: To look up a phone number by contact name quickly, use
questions.correctAnswer: Dictionary name→number

questions.prompt: If order matters and duplicates are allowed, choose a
questions.correctAnswer: List

questions.prompt: Which structure naturally expresses key-value settings?
questions.correctAnswer: Dictionary

tasks.name: Interfaces and Contracts
tasks.description: Specify function parameters, return values, and simple pre/postconditions.
tasks.genprompt: Generate MCQs (4 options) interpreting a simple function contract, choosing correct calls, and identifying input/return expectations.

questions.prompt: A function contract primarily states
questions.correctAnswer: What inputs it expects and what it returns
answers: What inputs it expects and what it returns

questions.prompt: Given add(a,b) returns a+b, which call is correct?
If a function promises to return an integer, then
questions.correctAnswer: Callers should expect an integer result
answers: Callers should expect an integer result

questions.prompt: A precondition describes

units.name: Test Programs

tasks.name: Testing Fundamentals

tasks.genprompt: Create MCQs (4 options) on why we test, defect vs. failure, expected vs. actual, pass/fail criteria, and reproducibility.

questions.prompt: The primary goal of software testing is to
questions.correctAnswer: Find defects before users do

questions.prompt: A defect (bug) is best described as
questions.correctAnswer: An error in the code or design that can cause failure
answers: An error in the code or design that can cause failure

questions.prompt: A test passes when

answers: The author says it is done

questions.prompt: A failure is when the software
questions.correctAnswer: Behaves differently from its specification

answers: Make the problem happen again with the same steps

questions.prompt: Which item belongs in a test result?
questions.correctAnswer: Observed actual output

questions.prompt: Testing early in the project usually
questions.correctAnswer: Reduces cost of fixing defects

questions.prompt: Which item is necessary to judge pass/fail?
questions.correctAnswer: A clear expected result

questions.prompt: Which best helps others fix a bug?
questions.correctAnswer: Exact steps to reproduce and observed output
answers: Exact steps to reproduce and observed output

answers: How the system handles invalid inputs

questions.prompt: A good introduction to a page should
questions.correctAnswer: Explain what the page covers and outcomes
answers: Explain what the page covers and outcomes

questions.prompt: Cross-linking related pages mainly
questions.correctAnswer: Improves navigation and reduces duplication

questions.prompt: Which item belongs in a page header/front matter for docs?
questions.correctAnswer: Title and brief summary

questions.prompt: Redundant content across pages should be handled by
questions.correctAnswer: Linking to a single source of truth
answers: Linking to a single source of truth

questions.prompt: The main benefit of consistent terminology is
questions.correctAnswer: Readers learn terms once and recognize them elsewhere
answers: Readers learn terms once and recognize them elsewhere

tasks.name: Procedures and Tutorials
tasks.description: Write accurate step-by-step instructions with clear outcomes and troubleshooting.
tasks.genprompt: Create MCQs (4 options) on writing steps, ordering, action verbs, expected results, warnings, tips, and troubleshooting entries.

questions.prompt: Good procedural steps typically start with
questions.correctAnswer: An action verb like Click or Enter
answers: An action verb like Click or Enter

questions.prompt: Each step should include
questions.correctAnswer: The action and expected result when needed
answers: The action and expected result when needed

questions.prompt: Numbered lists are best when
questions.correctAnswer: Order matters for the procedure

questions.prompt: A troubleshooting entry should
questions.correctAnswer: Name the symptom, likely cause, and fix
answers: Name the symptom, likely cause, and fix

questions.prompt: Warnings in procedures are used to
questions.correctAnswer: Prevent risky actions that could cause loss or harm
answers: Prevent risky actions that could cause loss or harm

questions.prompt: Placeholders like <USERNAME> should be
questions.correctAnswer: Explained so readers know what to substitute
answers: Explained so readers know what to substitute

questions.prompt: If a step may take several minutes, you should
questions.correctAnswer: Tell the reader the expected duration
answers: Say nothing to keep it short
Tell the reader the expected duration

questions.prompt: Screenshots in tutorials should
questions.correctAnswer: Highlight relevant areas and hide sensitive data
answers: Highlight relevant areas and hide sensitive data

questions.prompt: After completing steps, include a section that
questions.correctAnswer: Confirms expected end state or success criteria
answers: Confirms expected end state or success criteria

questions.prompt: When two paths are possible (Windows/Mac), you should
questions.correctAnswer: Separate steps clearly for each path
answers: Separate steps clearly for each path

tasks.name: Examples, References, and Style
tasks.description: Create helpful examples, concise references, and follow a style guide.
tasks.genprompt: Create MCQs (4 options) on minimal working examples, inputs/outputs, reference formatting, term definitions, and applying a style guide.

questions.prompt: A minimal working example is valuable because it
questions.correctAnswer: Shows the smallest complete case that works
answers: Shows the smallest complete case that works

questions.prompt: Good examples should include
questions.correctAnswer: Inputs, steps, and expected outputs

questions.prompt: A glossary entry should
questions.correctAnswer: Define a term in clear, simple language
answers: Define a term in clear, simple language

questions.prompt: Reference pages should prioritize
questions.correctAnswer: Accurate, concise facts and parameters

questions.prompt: A style guide helps documentation by
questions.correctAnswer: Keeping tone and formatting consistent

questions.prompt: When including command examples, you should
questions.correctAnswer: Clearly distinguish user input from output
answers: Clearly distinguish user input from output
Mix both in one line without labels

questions.prompt: Example data should be
questions.correctAnswer: Safe and non-sensitive

questions.prompt: When defining options/flags, include
questions.correctAnswer: What it does and valid values
answers: What it does and valid values

questions.prompt: If two terms mean the same thing, choose one and
questions.correctAnswer: Use it consistently across the docs
answers: Use both in the same sentence
Use it consistently across the docs

questions.prompt: Inline notes like Tip/Note/Warning are used to
questions.correctAnswer: Call attention to important details

tasks.name: Review, Accessibility, and Maintenance
tasks.description: Edit for clarity, check accessibility, and keep docs up to date with changes.
tasks.genprompt: Create MCQs (4 options) on peer review, plain language, alt text, color contrast, versioning, changelogs, and updating docs with releases.

questions.prompt: Peer review of docs mainly helps to
questions.correctAnswer: Catch unclear steps and errors

questions.prompt: Plain language means
questions.correctAnswer: Using simple, direct words the audience understands
answers: Using simple, direct words the audience understands

questions.prompt: Alternative text (alt text) for images should
questions.correctAnswer: Briefly describe the important content of the image
answers: Briefly describe the important content of the image

questions.prompt: Color-only cues in docs are risky because
questions.correctAnswer: Some readers may not perceive color differences
answers: Some readers may not perceive color differences

questions.prompt: When software changes, docs should be
questions.correctAnswer: Updated promptly to match new behavior
answers: Updated promptly to match new behavior

questions.prompt: Screenshots should be reviewed to
questions.correctAnswer: Remove or blur sensitive information

questions.prompt: A changelog helps readers by
questions.correctAnswer: Summarizing what changed between versions

questions.prompt: Link checking in docs is important to
questions.correctAnswer: Prevent broken navigation for readers

questions.prompt: Documentation version labels should match
questions.correctAnswer: The software version the docs describe
answers: The software version the docs describe

questions.prompt: The best time to review docs for accuracy is
questions.correctAnswer: Before release and after notable changes
answers: Before release and after notable changes
